M-062.NAME: onboarding-email-password-security
M-062.owns: Branded verification email UX, password-strength policy, password recovery, duplicate-email registration guidance, and user-facing pending-email copy.
M-062.purpose: Make email onboarding clearer and safer without changing deploy/install scripts or weakening verified-email ownership gates.

exports.fn-build-branded-verification-email: Render compact branded verification email HTML and text fallback for Resend delivery.
exports.fn-build-password-reset-email: Render password reset email content with one-time token semantics and safe text fallback.
exports.fn-validate-password-strength: Reject weak or common passwords consistently on backend and frontend.
exports.fn-request-password-reset: Accept a normalized email address and create a one-time reset flow without leaking reset tokens.
exports.fn-reset-password: Consume a valid reset token once and set a strong replacement password.
exports.view-registration-pending-email: Show concise check-email and spam-folder guidance after registration.

phase-44-scope.in: Verification email visual redesign, operator sender-avatar note, spam-folder hint, removal of stale 3-day trial/VPN wording, deletion of registration side-info panel, strong-password backend and frontend validation, forgot/reset password flow, duplicate-email UX with reset CTA, and rate-limited reset endpoints.
phase-44-scope.out: Changing install/deploy scripts, bypassing email verification, issuing access before email proof, guaranteeing sender avatar rendering inside every mailbox, or weakening MTProto/VPN provisioning guards.

constraints.constraint-1: Email verification and reset tokens must never be logged, returned after creation, or stored raw.
constraints.constraint-2: Password reset must be one-time, TTL-bound, rate-limited, and safe for already-authenticated and unauthenticated users.
constraints.constraint-3: Duplicate-email messaging is an approved UX tradeoff and must pair with throttling to limit account enumeration abuse.
constraints.constraint-4: Sender avatar behavior is mailbox/provider-side branding; KrotPN backend can improve templates and deliverability but cannot force recipient UI avatars through the Resend send API.

acceptance-metrics.metric-1: Registration pending view tells users to check spam and no longer mentions 3-day trial or immediate VPN access.
acceptance-metrics.metric-2: Registration and reset forms reject weak passwords consistently before and after API submission.
acceptance-metrics.metric-3: Forgot/reset flow works for valid tokens, rejects expired/replayed tokens, and returns safe generic request responses where appropriate.
acceptance-metrics.metric-4: Frontend build and Phase-44 smoke pass without touching deploy/install scripts.

M-062.hotspot: H-049: Password recovery and duplicate-email hints must not leak reset tokens, allow account takeover, or make email enumeration worse than the approved UX tradeoff
